etymology.description: 大 (big; great) with added 丶 to differentiate sound and meaning -> 太 (too big)
etymology.interpretation: The character 太 (tài, "too; very; extremely") uses 大 (dà, "big; great") as a base to convey a sense of augmentation. By adding 丶 (an extra dot), it differentiates from 大 to express something beyond the norm or limit, hence 'too; very; extremely'. It graphically conveys extending beyond, akin to emphasizing 'more than just big'.

examples.chinese: 这盘菜太辣了(這盤菜太辣了)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè pán cài tài là le.

examples.chinese: 她今天看起来太累了(她今天看起來太累了)。
examples.pinyin: Tā jīntiān kàn qǐlái tài lèi le.

examples.chinese: 外面的天气太好了(外面的天氣太好了)，我们出去走走吧(我們出去走走吧)！
examples.pinyin: Wàimiàn de tiānqì tài hǎo le, wǒmen chūqù zǒuzǒu ba!
examples.english: The weather outside is too good, let's go for a walk!
